---
title: "Match-Value Distributions in Long-Tail Consumption"
description: "Empirical work on the shape of match-value distributions using Steam playtime and Last.fm listening data."
date: "2026-05-01"
categories: [structural, recommender, long-tail]
draft: false
---

## Motivation

Standard discrete-choice models impose strong assumptions on the distribution of idiosyncratic match value. In long-tail consumption settings — games, music, video — these tail assumptions drive welfare implications and counterfactual predictions for recommender design.

## What I'm doing

Using Steam playtime and Last.fm listening histories as proxies for realized match value, I am characterizing the **higher moments** of the match-value distribution and asking whether common parametric families (EV1, normal) are adequate.

## Status

Early-stage; brainstorming and exploratory data analysis. Draft notes available on request.
